name: João Gilberto
description: Brazilian musician, pioneer of bossa nova (1931–2019)
abstract: João Gilberto do Prado Pereira de Oliveira ( ZHWOWN zhil-BAIR-too; Portuguese: [ʒuˈɐ̃w ʒiwˈbɛʁtu]; 10 June 1931 – 6 July 2019), known as João Gilberto,  was a Brazilian guitarist, singer, and composer who was a pioneer of the musical genre of bossa nova in the late 1950s. Around the world, he was often called the "father of bossa nova"; …

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And I was very friendly with Joao Gilberto. He used to borrow my guitar to do concerts.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: Joao Gilberto, by the way, is touring with us for these three weeks, to promote the new record that they had made but was not yet out, which was going to be “The Girl From Ipanema,” the big one.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: But when you have one artist like Joao Gilberto, just recently, that cost me a hundred thousand dollars 'cause he didn't get his visa straight in Brazil... we had to cancel the concert
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: That came to me through hearing the João Gilberto version of Desafinado... I was transfixed by this album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: in the sixties I was aware of Stan Getz and Astrud Gilberto and Joao Gilberto
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: Joao Gilberto. I got those records a long time ago, on the Odeon label.
